# UC-22.2.56 · NIS2 evidence integrity, certification and standards crosswalk control

## Description

Detects evidence packages or scheduled compliance searches whose cryptographic verification failed, whose scheduler runs were skipped, or whose clause metadata cannot be mapped to the ISO/IEC crosswalk lookup.

## Value

Gives assurance teams a single pane to prove that each regulatory clause still chains to a recognised international control—and that the exported proof files were not altered after creation—before an external auditor asks for the mapping spreadsheet.

## Implementation

Emit structured JSON lines into `index=audit_evidence` whenever exports run; include `sha256_expected` from secure storage. Mirror saved-search health from `scheduler` (consider Splunk Monitoring Console). Maintain `nis2_standards_crosswalk.csv` under GRC change control. Install Splunkbase 1928 if attestation lives in ServiceNow. Route failures to Records & Information owner.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
• Splunk Enterprise 9.x+ or Splunk Cloud: create restricted `index=audit_evidence` with only `grc_admins` RBAC role able to delete.
• HEC or scripted input that wraps your export pipeline and writes `sourcetype=evidence:export` JSON including `sha256_expected`, `sha256_observed`, `nis2_clause`.
• Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow ([Splunkbase 1928](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/1928)) if attestations sync from `sn_grc` tables; polling interval typically **300 seconds**.
• CSV lookup `nis2_standards_crosswalk.csv` versioned in Git with signing tag per release.
• Optional Splunk Enterprise Security ([Splunkbase 263](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/263)) to attach risk scores to broken evidence chains.

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Use **Settings > Server settings > HTTP Event Collector** to mint a token scoped exclusively to `audit_evidence`. For `scheduler` telemetry rely on default `_internal` ingestion—no extra input. Map ServiceNow fields `number`, `state`, `u_nis2_clause` via TA field transforms. Validate with `index=audit_evidence | head 1` showing all required keys.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Save SPL as `UC-22.2.56` hourly with `earliest=-2h`. Pipe notable rows to email or SOAR playbook. When ES is present, duplicate logic inside **Configure > Notable Event Aggregations** with aggregation field `evidence_id`.

```spl
(index=audit_evidence sourcetype=evidence:export) OR (index=_internal sourcetype=scheduler)
| eval nis2_clause=coalesce(nis2_clause, "Art.21(2)(a)")
| lookup nis2_standards_crosswalk.csv nis2_clause OUTPUT iso27001_control, iec62443_requirement, nist_csf
```

Understanding this SPL: union catches both content-level hash drift and platform-level scheduling health. The `lookup` enforces traceability to ISO/IEC language auditors expect. Use `where` clauses to isolate only unverifiable artefacts.

Step 3 — Validate
Run `| rest /servicesNS/-/-/saved/searches splunk_server=local count=0` to list production searches; confirm UC-tagged jobs exist. Spot-check one export: download file, compute SHA-256 externally, compare to event fields. Mismatch should fire within **60 minutes**.

Step 4 — Operationalize & Troubleshoot
Dashboard layout: row 1 hash integrity counts, row 2 crosswalk gaps, row 3 scheduler backlog. Runbook: if hash failure, rotate signing cert; if crosswalk gap, open GRC JIRA; if scheduler skip, inspect `splunkd.log` for `QuotaExceeded`. Use **Monitoring Console > Search > Scheduler** for macro view.

Step 5 — Governance
Quarterly attestation: legal reviews `nis2_standards_crosswalk.csv`. Archive prior versions with RFC3161 timestamps via `evidenceSigning`. Never suppress hash alerts without Records sign-off.

## SPL

```spl
(index=audit_evidence (sourcetype=evidence:saved_search OR sourcetype=evidence:export) earliest=-30d (hash_verified="false" OR isnull(hash_verified) OR match(lower(validation_note),"(fail|mismatch|expired)"))) OR (index=_internal sourcetype=scheduler earliest=-7d (status="skipped" OR status="deferred" OR like(reason,"%quota%")) savedsearch_name="UC-*")
| eval nis2_clause=coalesce(nis2_clause, obligation_clause, control_clause, "Art.21(2)(a)")
| lookup nis2_standards_crosswalk.csv nis2_clause OUTPUT iso27001_control, iec62443_requirement, nist_csf
| eval crosswalk_ok=if(isnotnull(iso27001_control) AND isnotnull(iec62443_requirement),1,0)
| where crosswalk_ok=0 OR hash_verified="false" OR isnull(hash_verified) OR status IN ("skipped","deferred")
| stats latest(_time) as last_seen latest(reason) as scheduler_reason values(sourcetype) as evidence_channels values(export_path) as paths by evidence_id nis2_clause iso27001_control iec62443_requirement
| sort - last_seen
```

## CIM SPL

```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Alerts where nodename=Alerts.All_Alerts by Alerts.signature | sort - count
```

## Visualization

Table listing evidence_id, nis2_clause, iso27001_control, iec62443_requirement, last_seen; sparkline of scheduler skips; single value for hash failures.
